commit.message:
refactor: switch MU-loader from whitelist to blacklist architecture (v6.1.0)

Load all plugins by default, only restrict known-heavy ecosystems
(WooCommerce, LearnPress, bbPress, etc.) when their page conditions
aren't met. Eliminates silent plugin breakage when new plugins are
installed. All restriction rules are now DB-driven via scanner.

commit.message:
Simplify ca() to direct claude passthrough

Positional args to claude CLI run in print mode which can't
execute tools or handle approvals. Making ca() a simple
passthrough lets users control the mode explicitly:
- ca         → interactive REPL (tools work)
- ca -p "q"  → explicit print mode

commit.message:
feat: Phase 3 complete — real substrate, v0.4.0 (113 tests)

Wire steward to steward-protocol substrate. Replace custom implementations
with real primitives. All Phase 3 gaps closed.

Core substrate:
- ServiceRegistry DI with 8 SVC_ constants (boot() wires everything)
- ToolRegistry from vibe_core (governance, safety, plugins)
- ToolSafetyGuard (Iron Dome: read-before-edit enforcement)
- MahaAttention (Lotus Router: O(1) PRIMARY tool dispatcher)
- ProviderChamber (MahaCellUnified cells, prana-ordered failover)
- Adapters: GoogleAdapter, MistralAdapter, AnthropicAdapter

New capabilities:
- Parallel tool execution via asyncio.gather() (3-phase: route→execute→process)
- InMemoryMemory (Chitta): tracks files_read/files_write per session
- EventBus (Narada): real-time event stream with Sudarshana rate limiting
- PromptContext: dynamic system prompt (git branch, timestamp injected)
- Smart context trim: tool results evicted first, compaction markers
- AnthropicAdapter: Claude provider support (tool_use blocks, input_schema)

Infrastructure:
- CLI: python -m steward "task", interactive REPL, --resume, --version
- SignalBus: AGENT_STARTUP, STATUS_UPDATE, AGENT_ERROR signals
- State persistence: Phoenix pattern (atomic write), .steward/session.json
- Token tracking: AgentUsage in done events, context budget warnings at 80%
- No Any in core code (AST-enforced by test_no_any.py)
- EditTool + GrepTool added to builtin tools

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: isolate e2e tests with dedicated database (#726) (#730)

* fix: isolate e2e tests with dedicated database to prevent data leaks (#726)

E2E tests were sharing the production corvid-agent.db, causing test data
(marketplace listings, reviews, agents) to persist and pollute the UI.

- Add DATABASE_PATH env var support to getDb() for external DB configuration
- Set DATABASE_PATH=e2e-test.db in playwright webServer env
- Add globalTeardown to delete the e2e DB (and WAL/SHM) after test runs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: respect DATABASE_PATH in backup route for e2e isolation

The backup endpoint hardcoded 'corvid-agent.db' as the source path,
but e2e tests now use DATABASE_PATH=e2e-test.db. Read the env var
so backups target the correct database file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add retry button for failed work tasks (#735)

* feat: add retry button for failed work tasks (#728)

- Add retryTask method to WorkTaskService (resets failed task via
  resetWorkTaskForRetry and re-executes from scratch)
- Add POST /api/work-tasks/:id/retry endpoint with operator/owner auth guard
- Add retry button to dashboard work task list (shown for failed tasks)
- Add retryTask to client WorkTaskService
- Register retry endpoint in OpenAPI route registry
- Add work_task_retry audit action type
- Add route handler tests for retry endpoint

Closes #728

to accommodate macOS CI runners that have variable scheduling latency.

commit.message:
Initial release — MLSys Accelerator by nyko.ai

Free interactive course to master ML Systems in 30 days, based on Harvard CS249r.
21 chapters with 5-step active learning pipeline (pre-test, briefing, exercise,
Feynman challenge, flashcards). Fully client-side, bilingual EN/FR, PWA-ready.

- All content pre-generated and code-split for offline use
- SM-2 spaced repetition for flashcards
- Mermaid diagrams in briefings, D3 knowledge map
- Progress persistence via localStorage with export/import
- Neural Terminal dark theme, mobile-first responsive design
- Stripe coffee link for community support

CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 — Based on Prof. Vijay Janapa Reddi's work (Harvard)

Errors surface via toast.

fix: switch from prompt hook to command hook for Stop event

Prompt hooks cannot make Claude continue working after block -
they just show an error and stop. Command hooks with {"decision": "block"}
format (like ralph-loop uses) properly re-engage Claude.

- New: session-end-checkpoint.sh (command hook, decision format)
- Deleted: session-end-learnings.sh (old static script)
- settings.json: type "prompt" -> type "command"

fix: number input UX — 0 silinebilir, leading zero yok

Input component type="number" için internal string state kullanır:
- Backspace/delete ile "0" silinebilir (userCleared tracking)
- "05" gibi leading zero'lar blur'da temizlenir
- Sadece sayısal karakterler kabul edilir (regex validation)
- Intermediate states ("3." gibi) bozulmaz
- Tüm type="number" Input kullanımları otomatik faydalanır (30+ input)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: Flatten project structure to root level

Move source code from nested refine-backlog/ to root-level directories
following standard patterns for bash projects:

Changes:
- bin/refine-backlog: Main CLI entry point (was refine-backlog/refine-backlog.sh)
- lib/: Library modules (was refine-backlog/lib/)
- scripts/init-refine-backlog: Setup script (was refine-backlog/init-refine-backlog.sh)
- skills/refine-backlog.md: Claude skill definition (was refine-backlog/skills/)

New additions:
- Makefile: Install, test, and clean targets
- Updated README: Installation via make install

Benefits:
- Standard project layout familiar to developers
- Cleaner navigation and import paths
- Works with package managers and standard install patterns
- No duplicate 'refine-backlog' directory naming

Updated all path references in:
- bin/refine-backlog (uses PROJECT_ROOT)
- scripts/init-refine-backlog (uses PROJECT_ROOT)
- Removed old refine-backlog/ directory

All tests and functionality unchanged, just reorganized.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add vector embedding and semantic search (feature-gated)

Add optional semantic search behind the `vector-search` Cargo feature
flag using fastembed (all-MiniLM-L6-v2, 23MB ONNX model). Includes:

- EmbeddingProvider trait with FastEmbedProvider implementation
- Binary embedding I/O with safe le-bytes serialization
- Content-hash diffing to skip unchanged nodes on rebuild
- Cosine similarity scoring with dimension/model validation
- `--semantic` CLI flag and `semantic` MCP param
- `--limit` flag for both keyword and semantic search
- 19 new unit tests using mock provider (no model download)

Default binary unchanged (~9MB). With `--features vector-search`: ~25-30MB.

Implements REQ-API-008

feat: support configurable CDP bind address

Chrome's remote debugging bind address. Enables Docker containers
to connect to CDP when set to 0.0.0.0.

Also add --remote-allow-origins=* for WebSocket connections from
non-localhost origins (e.g. Docker bridge network).

feat: Transform to OpenClaw Identity Trust - DID & Verifiable Credentials System

Complete transformation from video generator to W3C-compliant identity trust system.

## Major Changes

- Implement W3C DID Core 1.0 specification
  - Support for did:key, did:web, did:ethr methods
  - DID document generation and resolution
  - Ed25519 and secp256k1 cryptographic signatures

- Implement W3C Verifiable Credentials Data Model 1.1
  - Credential issuance with cryptographic proofs
  - Credential verification and trust validation
  - Support for credential expiration and revocation

- CLI Tool with 3 command aliases
  - openclaw-identity-trust (full command)
  - identity-trust (short command)
  - idt (minimal command)

- Agent Integration
  - 6 core tools for AI agents
  - Trust evaluation system
  - Policy-based access control

## Package Information

- Package name: openclaw-identity-trust
- Version: 1.0.0
- License: MIT
- Node.js: >= 18.0.0

## Technical Stack

- TypeScript 5.7 (100% type-safe)
- @noble/curves for cryptography
- Commander.js for CLI
- Local JSON storage (~/.openclaw/identity/)

## Standards Compliance

✅ W3C DID Core 1.0
✅ W3C Verifiable Credentials 1.1
✅ Ed25519 Signature 2020
✅ Multibase Encoding

## Testing Status

✅ DID creation and resolution
✅ Credential issuance and verification
✅ CLI commands functional
✅ Zero security vulnerabilities
✅ Build successful

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
